items.title: Johor police arrest 19‑year‑old, seize Mercedes after deadly five‑vehicle crash near Renggam
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 2 — Police have arrested a local man to assist in the investigation of an accident involving five vehicles that killed five people at Kilometre 27, Jalan Renggam–Simpang Renggam, Kluang. Kluang district police chief ACP Bahrin Mohd Noh said the 19-year-old man was believed to be driving a Mercedes Benz A250 at high speed and recklessly with a BMW 530e driven by a 22-year-old local man, who is also his elder brother. The driver of the Perodua Alza, a 43-year-old local man, he said suffered leg injuries, while the driver of the Proton Wira, a 22-year-old local man, was injured in the mouth.

items.title: Two found unconscious in house fire in Johor’s Taman Ehsan Jaya
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Two occupants of a two-storey terrace house were found unconscious after a fire broke out at Jalan Ehsan Jaya 3/10 in Taman Ehsan Jaya on Tuesday night. Johor Jaya Fire and Rescue Station operations commander Senior Fire Officer I Rafie Ahmad Sareng said a team was deployed after receiving an emergency call at about 9.06pm. He said the two victims were located unconscious in a bedroom upstairs by firefighters conducting rescue operations.

items.title: Nvidia CEO says has capacity to supply robust CPU and GPU growth
items.summary: TAIPEI, June 2 (Reuters) - Nvidia boss Jensen Huang said on Tuesday the company has enough supply to accommodate robust growth for central processing units (CPUs) as well as graphics processing units (GPUs) as it rides a boom in artificial intelligence. Chief Executive Huang was speaking at Nvidia's GTC press conference during Computex week in Taipei, a day after the $5 trillion chip giant unveiled a new chip that processes AI capabilities directly. Huang said the RTX Spark PC chip is part of Nvidia's effort with Microsoft to "reinvent the PC" for the AI era.

items.title: Dense trees and strong winds complicate helicopter mission in Tapah search for missing hiker
items.summary: TAPAH, June 2 — Clustered clouds, strong winds and the confined terrain surrounded by dense trees are among the main challenges faced by rescuers in the search and rescue (SAR) operation for a missing female hiker at Gunung Batu Putih here. Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant superintendent II Mohd Hasrizal Kamaruzzaman said the aerial operation, carried out using a Russian-made MI-17 helicopter from the JBPM Central Region Air Base yesterday, involved complex winching procedures to deploy and extract rescue personnel. He said the operation at the Sukaneka Camp area involved 32 rescuers.

items.title: High-speed luxury cars triggered deadly crash near Simpang Renggam that killed four family members, say Johor police
items.summary: KLUANG, June 2 — A four-member family was killed in a five-vehicle crash along Jalan Renggam–Kluang yesterday after two luxury cars were allegedly travelling at high speed and driving recklessly, police said. According to the New Straits Times , Kluang district police chief Assistant Commissioner Bahrin Mohd Noh said initial investigations indicated that a BMW 530e driven by a 22-year-old man was heading from Kluang towards Simpang Renggam when the incident occurred. The BMW driver later died while being taken to hospital.

items.title: Wall St ends higher, boosted by tech gains, US-Iran peace hopes
items.summary: US President Donald Trump said talks with Iran continue. Microsoft shares added 2.3 per cent. Qualcomm tumbled 8.8 per cent, while Intel fell 4.7 per cent.

items.title: Oil prices jump as Iran suspends peace talks
items.summary: NEW YORK: Oil prices jumped and equities were mixed Monday as Middle East peace talks stumbled, while Lebanese authorities said Hezbollah had accepted a US proposal for a "mutual cessation of attacks." Crude futures shot around seven per cent higher after an Iranian state news agency announced Tehran had suspended its negotiations with Washington via mediators. International benchmark Brent closed 4.2 per cent higher while its US counterpart, the West Texas Intermediate, added 5.5 per cent.

items.title: Suspects with drug records arrested in Ipoh after viral Telekom cable theft video surfaces
items.summary: IPOH, June 2 — Police have arrested two men suspected of attempting to steal Telekom Malaysia cables in Taman Rapat Setia Baru here, after a video of the incident went viral on social media. Ipoh police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said the suspects, aged 34 and 38, were arrested around 1am yesterday at a restaurant in Bercham. “Yesterday, police detected a viral video on X showing two suspicious men opening a Telekom Malaysia distribution box (DB) near a house in Taman Rapat Setia Baru.

items.title: Oil steadies as uncertainty over US-Iran talks keeps markets on edge
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Oil prices held on to most of the previous session's sharp gains in early trade on Tuesday on uncertainty over the status of ceasefire talks between the United States and Iran and the potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. US President Donald Trump said on Monday talks with Iran were ongoing, while Tasnim news agency reported that Teheran had suspended indirect negotiations with Washington. Brent crude futures inched up 6 cents, or 0.06 per cent, to US$95.04 a barrel at 0001 GMT, while US West Texas Intermediate fell 17 cents, or 0.18 per cent, to $91.99 a barrel.

items.title: Denmark's PM Mette Frederiksen, tenacious and tough on migration
items.summary: SHE has stood up to Donald Trump and flaunts her cooking skills on Instagram: Mette Frederiksen, who will stay on for a third term as Denmark's prime minister after clinching a coalition deal on Monday, mixes high-stakes international politics with Danes' everyday concerns. The party stalwart is seen as the embodiment of Denmark's modernised social democracy, embracing a strict migration policy in order to defend the country's cherished welfare state. But Frederiksen doesn't just embody authority.

items.title: Anwar: Govt to bolster targeted aid, RON95 subsidy reforms as cost pressures persist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said the government will continue refining targeted assistance measures and other support programmes to help Malaysians cope with rising living costs while preserving economic stability. According to The Star , Anwar said Malaysia is operating in an increasingly challenging environment shaped by economic uncertainty, climate-related concerns, rapid advances in artificial intelligence and evolving geopolitical developments. Speaking at the investiture ceremony held in conjunction with the official birthday celebration of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, Anwar said efforts to address cost-of-living concerns include fiscal reforms, the targeted RON95 fuel subsidy mechanism and the Budi Madani programme.

items.title: EC chairman says Johor election schedule to follow Speaker’s official notice on dissolution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — The Election Commission (EC) will hold a Special Meeting for the 16th Johor state assembly elections as soon as it receives official notification from the Speaker of the Johor assembly on the date of the dissolution. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the special meeting would discuss the key dates for the state polls. “The EC takes note of the press conference by Johor Mentri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi regarding the dissolution of the 15th Johor State Legislative Assembly on June 1, 2026.

items.title: Australia says wheat crop set to plunge
items.summary: SYDNEY: Australia's wheat crop is set to fall by more than a quarter this season, a government report said Tuesday, as farmers face dry conditions and a surge in fuel and fertiliser prices fed by conflict in the Middle East. A major world wheat exporter, Australia's harvest this season is set to plunge by 26 per cent from last year to 26.7 million tonnes, according to the agriculture department's quarterly assessment. The forecast wheat crop is 23 per cent below the five-year average and eight per cent below the 10-year average, the government report said.

items.title: Speeding cars caused crash that killed family of 4, say police
items.summary: District police chief Assistant Commissioner Bahrin Mohd Noh said initial investigations found that a BMW 530e driven by a 22-year-old man was travelling from Kluang towards Simpang Renggam at high speed. The BMW driver died while being rushed to hospital. The 43-year-old Alza driver sustained leg injuries, while the 22-year-old Proton Wira driver suffered injuries to his mouth.

items.title: Pro-Trump lawyer, leftist senator launch Colombia runoff campaigns
items.summary: BOGOTÁ: The hard-right Donald Trump fan and leftist senator vying to become president of Colombia traded insults on Monday as they threw themselves into campaigning for the June 21 election runoff. Right-wing lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella was the surprise winner of the first round of the presidential election on Sunday, held against the backdrop of a surge in drug-related guerrilla violence. Cepeda, an acolyte of polarizing left-wing President Gustavo Petro, campaigned on pursuing the struggling peace process and expanding social programs.

items.title: Israel intercepts projectiles launched from Lebanon
items.summary: JERUSALEM: The Israeli military said early Tuesday air defences intercepted two projectiles that crossed from Lebanon into northern Israel, hours after US President Donald Trump said fighting would end. "Following the sirens that sounded at 01.35 (2235 GMT) in several areas in northern Israel, the IAF intercepted two projectiles that crossed from Lebanon into Israeli territory," the military said on Telegram. It also said it identified a "suspicious aerial target" that later fell in Israeli territory near the Lebanese border, adding that no injuries were reported.--AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Trump: Israel, Hizbollah agree to halt fighting
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said Monday that Israel and Hizbollah had agreed to stop fighting, adding that Iran talks were moving rapidly after seeming to falter over Israel's Lebanon offensive. Trump said he had spoken to Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and had a "very good call" through unnamed representatives with the Hizbollah group. Netanyahu agreed to call off a military raid on Beirut while Hizbollah agreed "all shooting will stop," Trump said on his Truth Social network.

items.title: Why not a happy palm oil day?
items.summary: Palm oil, too, deserves a better story. So why not palm oil? Why not a Happy Palm Oil Day?

items.title: Kenanga Research lowers earnings forecast for NationGate in FY26
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kenanga Research has lowered its earnings forecast for NationGate Holdings Bhd by 20 per cent to RM80 million for financial year 2026 (FY26) on a lower margin assumption of 1.3 per cent. However, the firm raised its FY27 forecast by 11 per cent on the back of new customers from networking that will kick in next year. The firm said that NationGate's first quarter adjusted earnings of RM0.1 million came in below the firm's and street's forecasts of RM100 million and RM106 million respectively for FY26.

items.title: Cement prices to stay firm, but upside seen limited
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Cement prices are expected to be firmer in the near term, benefitting industry players such as Malayan Cement Bhd. Based on industry checks by Affin Hwang Investment Bank Bhd, the average selling price (ASP) for bulk cement rose to about RM410 per tonne in the quarter ended March 31 2026 from RM390 per tonne in the preceding quarter. While the latest price increase supports earnings, Affin Hwang does not expect further meaningful upside to cement ASPs in the near term.

items.title: Gold Li still focused on Johor's residential markets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Gold Li Holdings Bhd is entering its next phase of growth with a structured development pipeline that provides visibility for future earnings. Gold Li, in an email interview, said over the next one to two years, project launches will be rolled out progressively, supported by existing approvals, market demand and construction readiness. Gold Li said its business model is built around its position as a focused and agile property developer with 27 years of operating experience.

items.title: Malaysia's e-commerce: The race beyond price war
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's e-commerce industry is moving beyond the era of relentless price wars as platforms increasingly compete on trust, fulfilment reliability and operational efficiency. The shift comes as competition from China-linked platforms intensifies across Southeast Asia, forcing established marketplaces to strengthen governance, logistics capabilities and customer experience while protecting margins and seller economics. [[nid:1453381]] Yap said the trend reflects broader changes in consumer behaviour as Malaysia's digital economy matures.

items.title: Guterres stresses need for UN force in Lebanon after UNIFIL mandate ends
items.summary: UNITED NATIONS, United States: UN chief Antonio Guterres said Monday that it will be necessary to maintain peacekeepers in Lebanon after the mandate of the current mission expires at year-end. Concerns over the exit of the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) come with Israeli troops occupying south Lebanon's border areas, and as Israel and Lebanon hold direct negotiations seeking to end decades of hostilities. UNIFIL currently counts some 7,500 peacekeepers from nearly 50 countries.

items.title: Teens adjust online habits as under-16 social media rules take effect [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Teenagers are adjusting to the new age-verification requirement for social media users under 16 years old, with some planning to change their online habits and others welcoming closer parental control. Muhammad Irfan Nazhan, 13, expects to spend more time on outdoor activities. Social media content creator and parent Daphne Iking said: "The children have screen time limits.

items.title: Mexican police tear-gas teachers' protest 10 days before World Cup
items.summary: MEXICO CITY: Mexico City police hurled tear gas at protesting teachers to keep them from reaching the historic square where the "Fan fest" for the 2026 World Cup is under construction, according to AFP journalists. The clash started when teachers broke through one of the metal barriers that have been set up at the entrance to the Zocalo plaza, a block from the government palace and a giant screen where fans will watch Mexico's first World Cup game on June 11. Hundreds of riot police guarded the square behind tall temporary metal barricades.

items.title: Experts: Malaysia likely to recover NSM payments through arbitration [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's efforts to recover payments made for the cancelled Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system are likely to proceed through arbitration, with experts saying reclaiming funds already disbursed may be possible. A lawyer and a defence analyst said the key issue now was not whether funds could be recovered, but how far any claim could extend beyond direct payments already made. Lawyer Matthew Thomas Philip said the outcome would depend on the terms of the contract and the legal framework agreed by both parties.

items.title: What are fentanyl, synthetic cannabinoids and why is Malaysia sounding the alarm?
items.summary: Nada director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh noted that many users locally believe they are consuming psilocybin — the compound found in so-called magic mushrooms — when they are actually inhaling synthetic cannabinoids. Firstly, what is fentanyl? Fentanyl comes in two forms.

items.title: High costs and low awareness slowing household solar adoption in Kelantan, researcher says
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 2 — Despite its significant geographical and climatic potential to become the nation’s renewable energy (RE) hub or economic hotspot, Kelantan has seen slow adoption of renewable energy technology at the household and small and medium enterprise (SME) levels. Associate Professor Hasyiya Karimah Adli of the Faculty of Data Science and Computing, Universiti Malaysia Kelantan (UMK), said that although large-scale solar projects have begun to emerge in the state, public awareness and uptake of private solar systems remain low due to the high upfront capital costs. “Kelantan’s relatively flat terrain, coupled with consistent sunlight exposure and high solar radiation levels ranging from 4.21 to 5.56 kWh/m² per day, makes the state highly suitable for the solar industry.

items.title: Court order halts Trump-backed US$1.8bil compensation fund
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The Justice Department said Monday it will abide by a court order temporarily freezing a US$1.8 billion compensation package that critics have denounced as a "slush fund" for President Donald Trump's political allies. The move comes amid US media reports that the Trump administration plans to scrap plans for the fund, which has come in for fierce criticism by Democrats and even some members of Trump's Republican Party. According to Axios and other US news outlets, the Trump administration plans to drop the fund.

items.title: Perak may impose stricter rules to ensure hikers' safety
items.summary: THE Perak government will review the standard operating procedure (SOP) for hiking and forest-entry activities to enhance safety of visitors. State Science, Environment and Green Technology Committee chairman Teh Kok Lim said stricter measures could be introduced following the SOP review. He said while the current SOP was generally adequate, its effectiveness depended largely on compliance and cooperation from all parties, particularly hikers.

items.title: Denmark PM Frederiksen forms new coalition government
items.summary: COPENHAGEN: Denmark's Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen said Monday she had managed to form a left-leaning minority coalition government following months of talks after inconclusive elections in March. A statement from the palace said the coalition would consist of Frederiksen's Social Democrats, the Socialist People's Party, the centre-left Radikale Venstre and the centrist Moderates. Frederiksen said she would present her political programme on Tuesday, and on Wednesday the new cabinet.

items.title: One-month deadline to save data
items.summary: SOCIAL MEDIA users under 16 years old have one month to transfer their data before their accounts are restricted or suspended, said the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC). MCMC said social media platforms are required to carry out age verification for existing users within six months. "The CPC requires licensed social media platforms to implement child-safety-by-design measures and ensure age-appropriate protections," said MCMC.

items.title: Google parent Alphabet to raise US$80bil to fuel AI expansion
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO: Google parent Alphabet announced Monday it plans to raise up to US$80 billion in stock to fund a major expansion of its artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure, with Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway committing US$10 billion as part of the deal. The fundraising comes as Alphabet ramps up investment spending, with capital expenditures expected to reach US$180-US$190 billion in 2026 and set to rise further in 2027. To help close that gap, Alphabet said it plans to raise the new funds through a combination of a US$30 billion public stock offering, a US$10 billion private sale to Berkshire Hathaway, and a further US$40 billion share sale programme to be launched in the third quarter.

items.title: Modenas introduces three new models
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Motosikal dan Enjin Nasional Sdn Bhd (Modenas), a subsidiary of DRB-HICOM Bhd, has expanded its motorcycle lineup with three new models at the Malaysia Bike Show 2026 in Serdang. The new models are the Modenas Z650 S, Kawasaki Z1100 SE and Kawasaki Ninja ZX-10R. Roslan said the three models would strengthen Modenas' portfolio, catering to daily commuters and performance-oriented enthusiasts.

items.title: Trump: Iran talks moving fast despite threat to widen war
items.summary: TEHERAN: US President Donald Trump said Monday that talks with Iran were moving at a "rapid pace", even as Teheran threatened to widen the war by keeping the Strait of Hormuz blocked and activating other pressure points around the region. Iran's Tasnim news agency reported that Teheran had suspended dialogue with mediators over Israel's expanding offensive in Lebanon, where the US ally again threatened strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs. Trump later said he had persuaded Israel and Iran-backed Hizbollah to de-escalate, with Lebanon's US embassy saying the group had accepted a US proposal for a "mutual cessation of attacks."

items.title: China patrols waters near Taiwan
items.summary: CHINA's Coast Guard said yesterday it conducted "law enforcement" patrols in waters east of Taiwan in response to plans by Japan and the Philippines to begin maritime boundary delimitation talks in areas China claims fall under its jurisdiction. China's Coast Guard said a flotilla carried out the patrol in accordance with the law in those waters. Taiwan's Coast Guard had no immediate comment.

items.title: EU proposes to break up with US tech amid fear of American 'kill switch'
items.summary: WARY of being vulnerable to the whims of foreign governments, the European Union is preparing far-reaching new moves to ditch American digital companies and Chinese chips in favour of European alternatives. The hotly awaited package of new rules on chips, cloud computing and artificial intelligence (AI) will be presented tomorrow as part of the EU's effort to "reclaim its place in the global race for geoeconomic power", said a draft document seen by AFP. Top EU officials don't directly name their target as the US, but American tech dominates, from cloud computing to social media to e-commerce.

items.title: AI chip boom may spark regional economic renaissance
items.summary: INVESTORS are piling into South Korea and Taiwan for semiconductor stocks, but the artificial intelligence (AI) boom isn't just fattening profits at North Asia's chip champions. Real gross domestic product growth for two of North Asia's AI powerhouses — Taiwan and South Korea — has risen over the past 12 to 15 months, FactSet data shows, with Taiwan's now running at 11.8 per cent in the first quarter. South Korea's SK hynix and Samsung Electronics — two of the world's biggest chipmakers — now tie bonuses directly to operating profit.

items.title: New York Times publisher slams AI companies' 'brazen theft' from news outlets
items.summary: MARSEILLE: The New York Times publisher on Monday slammed artificial intelligence companies for "brazen theft of intellectual property", warning they threaten the future of journalism during a speech at the World News Media Congress in the French city of Marseille. products – a brazen theft of intellectual property that has occurred at an unprecedented scale. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Defence minister: Norway needs to be held responsible for missile export cancellation, Malaysia’s right to ask for compensation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Malaysia has the right to claim appropriate compensation from Norway and the company involved, following the cancellation of the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) export licence, said Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. He said the claim is justified given that Malaysia has suffered financial losses, as well as lost valuable time and opportunity as a result of the cancellation. If Norway had informed us earlier about the export licence cancellation, those funds could have been redirected to other purposes,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Johor Pakatan says prepared for state polls following dissolution of state assembly
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 1 — Johor Pakatan Harapan (PH) is prepared to face the upcoming state election following the dissolution of the state legislative assembly. In a joint statement, Johor PH said the coalition had expected that Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) would dissolve the assembly soon to pave way for an election. The statement was co-signed by Johor PH chairman and Johor Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) chief Aminolhuda Hassan; Johor PKR chief Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa; and Johor DAP chief Teo Nie Ching.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain alert issued for six states until 9pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds expected to affect several areas in six states until 9 pm today. Meanwhile, in Sabah, the warning covers the Interior division (Nabawan) and Sandakan division (Telupid, Beluran and Sandakan). In Sarawak, the affected areas are Kuching, Serian, Samarahan, Sri Aman, Betong (Betong), Sibu (Kanowit), Kapit (Song and Bukit Mabong), Miri (Beluru and Telang Usan), and Limbang.

items.title: PM Anwar expected at Sarawak state-level Gawai Day celebration on June 21
items.summary: KUCHING, June 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is expected to attend this year’s Sarawak state-level Gawai Day celebrations to be held here on June 21, said Sarawak Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas. He added that the Yang Dipertua Negeri of Sarawak Tun Dr Wan Junaidi Tuanku Jaafar will be the guest of honour at the event, which is also expected to be attended by Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg and Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadilah Yusof. He told reporters this at the 2026 Gawai Dayak Day Open House at the Unit for Other Religions (UNIFOR) Complex here today.

items.title: Police on the trail of three suspects involved in armed robbery cases in Pekan Gurney
items.summary: IPOH, June 1 — Police are hunting for three men, believed to be Indonesian nationals, involved in a gang robbery armed with machetes at a house in the Pekan Gurney area, Manjung yesterday. Manjung District Police Chief, ACP Hasbullah Abd Rahman, said that his department received a report regarding the incident from a female victim in her 40s at 6.27am. “In the incident, three male suspects, believed to be Indonesian nationals, entered the house and threatened the victim with a machete before fleeing with cash and jewelry.

items.title: Johor DAP: Marina Ibrahim not offered GLC post, but position with statutory body involved in welfare
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 1 — Johor DAP has clarified that the party had offered to appoint incumbent Skudai assemblyman Marina Ibrahim to helm a statutory body and not a government-linked company (GLC) as seen in a letter that went viral. Johor DAP vice-chairman Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali said there is a difference in a statutory body and a GLC. He said Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching had stated that she offered Marina to lead a statutory body and not a GLC.

items.title: Five hurt in five‑vehicle collision in Kudat
items.summary: KUDAT, June 1 — Five people were injured in a road accident involving five vehicles at Jalan Kampung Kandawayon here late yesterday. Kudat Fire and Rescue Station chief Darikson Galanang said the crash involved a Perodua Axia, a Proton, a Toyota Revo and two Perodua Myvi cars. Two other injured victims, aged 24 and 26, were the driver and passenger of a grey Myvi.

items.title: Johor state assembly dissolved, Onn Hafiz cites need for mandate to drive development
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 1 — The Johor state legislative assembly has been dissolved, paving the way for the state’s upcoming election. Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the dissolution after obtaining the consent of Johor Regent Tunku Mahkota Ismail Sultan Ibrahim earlier today. The previous Johor state election was held in March 2022.

items.title: Man missing, feared drowned after boat overturns near SK Nanga Balang in Kapit
items.summary: KANOWIT, June 1 — A 32-year-old man is feared drowned after a boat carrying seven passengers capsized near SK Nanga Balang in Gaat, Kapit, this morning. The victim, identified as Spielberg Dylan Sekunyir, was reported missing following the incident, while six other passengers managed to reach safety. A spokesperson for the department said firefighters from the Kapit Fire and Rescue Station (BBP Kapit) were dispatched to the scene after first proceeding to the Kapit police station to obtain information on the incident.

items.title: Public rescue injured driver, passenger after crash near SMK Sematan, says Sarawak Fire Dept
items.summary: KUCHING, June 1 — A man was seriously injured in a road accident involving a sedan in front of Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Sematan yesterday afternoon. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) Operations Centre said the injured man was the driver of the vehicle involved in the accident. The male driver was not trapped but sustained serious injuries, while a female passenger also suffered injuries.

items.title: Family among four killed in Toyota Vios as BMW, Mercedes, Wira and Alza collide near Simpang Renggam Prison
items.summary: KLUANG, June 1 — Four people were killed and two others injured in a collision involving five vehicles along Jalan Renggam–Simpang Renggam, in front of Simpang Renggam Prison, this afternoon. The crash, which occurred at about 1pm, involved a Perodua Alza, Proton Wira, Mercedes Benz, BMW and Toyota Vios, Harian Metro reported. Four occupants of the Mercedes Benz also survived: Mohd Azhar Ahmad, 63; his son Muhammad Hariz Mohd Azhar, 23; Rahimar A Rahman, 57; and a female passenger whose identity is still unknown.

items.title: Fahmi: Malaysians must heed King’s call to uphold Rukun Negara, preserve unity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Politicians and Malaysians in general should heed and uphold the call by His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, for the people to embrace and practise the spirit and values of the Rukun Negara in their daily lives, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. “I respectfully uphold His Majesty’s call, and I believe politicians across the country will do the same. Earlier, Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, graced the ceremony.

items.title: Johor assembly dissolution speculation mounts as Onn Hafiz set to hold 5pm press conference
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, June 1 — All eyes are on Johor today amid speculation that the state assembly could be dissolved. According to the New Straits Times , Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi is scheduled to hold a special press conference at his official residence at 5pm. The announcement, eagerly awaited across the political divide, is expected to pave the way for state polls.

items.title: Fahmi: Social media platforms must respect Malaysian laws as age‑verification begins
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Social media platforms operating in Malaysia must respect and comply with the country’s laws, particularly those relating to online safety and the protection of children, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. He said the reminder comes as the government begins enforcing age-verification requirements for social media account registration from today through the implementation of the Child Protection Code (CPC) and Risk Mitigation Code (RMC). Fahmi said the two codes, introduced under the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA), represent a crucial first step in protecting individuals under the age of 16 from online harm.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues alert for thunderstorms, heavy rain in seven states until 5pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit seven states until 5pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). In a weather warning issued at 3.15 pm, MetMalaysia said the affected areas in Perak are Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, and Kuala Kangsar. In Selangor, the warning covers Hulu Langat and Sepang, while in Negeri Sembilan it involves Jelebu, Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah and Rembau.

items.title: BN at 52: Zahid Hamidi rallies coalition for unity, renewal and resilience ahead polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has called for mature politics, responsible leadership and practical solutions to address current challenges in an increasingly complex global environment. In conjunction with BN’s 52nd anniversary, Ahmad Zahid, who is also Deputy Prime Minister, said the coalition must continue to move forward with a spirit of renewal, grounded in experience and an openness to adapt to future demands. Ahmad Zahid, who is Umno president, said BN was formed 52 years ago out of a spirit of understanding, moderation and cooperation among Malaysia’s diverse communities, to build the nation collectively.

items.title: Kelantan JPJ: BMW 320i confiscated over fake road tax, five speeding summonses uncovered
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 1 — The Kelantan Road Transport Department (JPJ) seized a luxury BMW 320i on suspicion of displaying a fake road tax document during a special motorcycle operation held in conjunction with the Hari Raya Aidiladha Operations (HRAA) yesterday. Kelantan JPJ director Mohd Misuari Abdullah said that checks using the Smart Enforcement Device (SmED) found that the vehicle’s road tax had actually expired in July 2025, while the fake copy indicated that it was valid until August 2026. “The vehicle was seized under Section 108 of the Road Transport Act 1987 for allegedly displaying a forged document,” he told reporters after the HRAA 2026 Special Motorcycle Operation at the Malaysian Road Transport Academy (Eastern Region) here.

items.title: GOF joins helicopter‑deployed teams in Gunung Batu Putih search for Jaslinda Saludin missing May 23
items.summary: He said the GOF personnel, together with eight members of JBPM’s Special Tactical Operation and Rescue Team of Malaysia (STORM), eight Forestry Department personnel and mountain guides, were flown into the search area aboard an MI-17 helicopter, significantly reducing travel time compared with previous overland routes through Gunung Batu Bujang and Pos Kuala Woh. Meanwhile, Perak JBPM director Datuk Sayani Saidon said SAR efforts remain focused on the area between Kem Kantoi and Gunung Rayu, involving personnel from JBPM, the Forestry Department, mountain guides and the GOF. He said SAR operations were continuing in the area between Kem Kantoi and Gunung Rayu involving personnel from JBPM, the Forestry Department, Forestry Mountain Guides and the GOF.

items.title: Nine‑year‑old boy dies after fall from 13th‑floor apartment in Ampang, police probe negligence
items.summary: Ampang Jaya police chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said they received information regarding the incident from a woman at about 7.45pm after she found the victim unconscious. He said the boy was confirmed dead at the scene by an assistant medical officer from the Ampang Hospital. Following the incident, Khairul Anuar said police have recorded statements from five individuals, comprising the boy’s parents and family members as well as witnesses.

items.title: Puad Zarkashi demands DAP disclosure of GLC posts as Marina Ibrahim’s retirement sparks political uproar
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, June 1 — Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi has accused DAP of using government‑linked company (GLC) positions as political bargaining chips, following controversy over a purported offer involving Skudai assemblyman Marina Ibrahim. Puad claimed the matter came to light when Marina allegedly rejected a proposal to contest the Tiram state seat in exchange for a GLC post. “DAP is worse.

items.title: Taxpayers can now file assessment appeals online through MyTax and track status in real time, says IRB
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 1 — Taxpayers can now file assessment appeals or apply for an extension of time to appeal tax assessments raised under the Income Tax Act 1967 through the new e-Assessment Appeal service on the MyTax portal. “Among the new experiences for taxpayers include the ability to submit assessment appeals or applications for extension of time from anywhere and in real time,” the IRB said. For access, the IRB said individual taxpayers may submit appeals or extension applications via the individual taxpayer role on MyTax.
